summary: Tested threaded comments/reply system on recruitment posts. All 16 backend tests passed. Frontend UI testing confirmed comment modal, threaded replies, like buttons, and reply functionality all working correctly.
critical_code_review_comments: Comment system correctly implements one-level deep nesting (replies to top-level comments only)
GET comments endpoint properly nests replies under parent's 'replies' array
is_liked field correctly returned for authenticated users on both comments and replies
Comment likes use separate collection 'recruitment_comment_likes' - good separation
replies_count on parent comment increments when reply is added
Comments sorted by created_at descending (newest first) as expected
seed_data_creation: Created test comments with TEST_ prefix during testing
context_for_next_testing_agent: Threaded comment system fully tested and working. Backend endpoints: POST /api/recruitment/posts/{postId}/comment (with optional parent_id for replies), GET /api/recruitment/posts/{postId}/comments (returns threaded structure), POST /api/recruitment/comments/{commentId}/like (toggles like). Frontend CommentModal renders replies indented (ml-10) under parent comments with Reply and Like buttons.

create_comment.notes: Requires auth, creates top-level comment with text field

create_reply.notes: Creates reply nested under parent comment, increments parent's replies_count

get_threaded_comments.notes: Returns threaded structure with replies array nested under each parent

like_comment.notes: Toggles like, returns liked boolean and likes_count

is_liked_field.notes: Comments include is_liked field for authenticated users

frontend_comment_modal.notes: Modal opens on comment icon click, shows threaded comments

frontend_reply_button.notes: Reply button shows 'Replying to X' indicator, posts reply nested under parent

frontend_like_button.notes: Heart icon toggles fill and count on click

frontend_comment_input.notes: Input field with send button works correctly
